findings.evidence: PR lines 105 and 127 define only `instSemilatticeSup` and `finset_sup_toSubmodule`; Mathlib provides the needed general submodule induction at `mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Span/Basic.lean:328` (`Submodule.iSup_induction`) and finite-support membership at `mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Finsupp/Span.lean:109`.
findings.fix: Define `SupSet`/`CompleteSemilatticeSup` for `Subcoalgebra` with underlying submodule `sSup` or `iSup`, prove `iSup_toSubmodule`, and derive the binary and Finset API from it.
findings.issue: `Subcoalgebra` only gets `SemilatticeSup`, with finite joins built afterward, but the same stability argument works for arbitrary `iSup`/`sSup` of subcoalgebras.

findings.evidence: PR line 150 states only `finset_sup_finite`; Mathlib already has the corresponding general submodule lemma `Submodule.fg_iSup` at `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Finiteness/Basic.lean:55`.
findings.fix: Add the general theorem `Module.Finite R (⨆ i, D i).toSubmodule` under `[Finite ι]` and `∀ i, Module.Finite R (D i).toSubmodule`, then keep the Finset lemma as a corollary if needed.
findings.issue: `finset_sup_finite` is a Finset-special statement, but once joins are at the natural `iSup` level the finiteness result should be stated for any finite index type.

summary: The join API stops at binary/Finset joins even though the construction naturally supports arbitrary suprema, so the API is below the Mathlib lattice level.
